---
title: "TODO: Incorporate Common Pitfalls Throughout the Handbook"
---

The archived `common-pitfalls.Qmd` chapter contained 20+ pitfalls covering:

1. Association vs causation
2. Post-treatment variable adjustment
3. Targeting the wrong estimand
4. Insufficient confounder adjustment
5. Positivity violations / extreme weights
6. Immortal time bias
7. Selection bias / collider conditioning
8. Table-2 fallacy
9. Non-collapsibility of odds ratios
10. Ignoring time-varying confounding
11. Healthy adherer / healthy user bias
12. Missing data treated as MCAR
13. Competing risks treated as censoring
14. Overfitting / regularization in causal models
15. Misinterpreting sensitivity analyses
16. Ignoring measurement error
17. Ecological fallacy
18. p-hacking / garden of forking paths
19. Inappropriate subgroup analysis
20. Not pre-specifying the analysis

**Action needed:** Review each pitfall and ensure the key lesson is incorporated
as a concrete warning, callout, or example in the relevant method chapter rather
than being isolated in a standalone pitfalls chapter. Many of these are already
partially addressed throughout the handbook. The goal is to make sure none are
missed entirely.

Priority pitfalls to verify are covered:
- Immortal time bias (should be in Ch 1.2 or case study)
- Post-treatment adjustment (should be in g-comp or IPTW)
- Table-2 fallacy (should be in Ch 1.1)
- Non-collapsibility (should be in Ch 1.1 or IPTW)
- Healthy adherer bias (should be in longitudinal chapters)
- Competing risks as censoring (should be in Ch 3.11)
